text:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST AND MRI BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT GADOLINIUM CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION: 56-year-old female with known Stage IV BRAF V600E-mutant metastatic cutaneous melanoma, currently on nivolumab 480 mg IV every 4 weeks maintenance monotherapy (following ipilimumab/nivolumab combination induction complicated by grade 3 immune-mediated colitis, requiring ipilimumab discontinuation and high-dose corticosteroids). Prior to combination immunotherapy, patient had received dabrafenib/trametinib as first-line therapy with initial partial response before progression at approximately 8 months. Study performed for interval disease assessment. Prior comparison: CT chest/abdomen/pelvis dated approximately 10 weeks after initiation of ipilimumab/nivolumab; MRI brain from same date.

TECHNIQUE:
CT Chest/Abdomen/Pelvis: Multidetector CT acquisition through the chest, abdomen, and pelvis following intravenous administration of iohexol 350 mg/mL (100 mL). Images reconstructed in axial, coronal, and sagittal planes at 2.5 mm slice thickness. Oral contrast administered. No known contrast allergy on file. Creatinine reviewed and within acceptable parameters prior to contrast administration.

MRI Brain: Multiplanar, multisequence MRI of the brain performed at 3.0 Tesla. Sequences obtained include: axial T1-weighted pre- and post-gadolinium (gadobutrol 0.1 mmol/kg IV), axial T2-weighted, axial FLAIR, axial DWI with ADC mapping, axial gradient echo susceptibility-weighted imaging (SWI), coronal T1 post-contrast, sagittal T1 post-contrast. Technique optimized for metastasis detection using thin-slice 1 mm isotropic MP-RAGE post-gadolinium reconstruction.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
FINDINGS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

CT CHEST:

Lungs and Airways: The lungs are clear. The two previously identified small pulmonary nodules in the right lower lobe -- the larger of which had measured 0.3 cm and the smaller 0.2 cm on the most recent prior CT -- are no longer perceptible on the current examination. Given the conspicuity threshold of current-generation CT and the size trajectory, these are considered to be below the current limits of reliable CT detection and may represent complete resolution. No new pulmonary nodules, consolidation, or ground-glass opacities are identified. The airways are patent to the level of the subsegmental bronchi. No pleural effusions. No pneumothorax.

Mediastinum and Hila: The mediastinum is midline. There is no significant mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy. No pericardial effusion. Heart size is within normal limits.

Axillary Regions: The right axillary lymph node previously measuring 0.9 cm in short-axis diameter now measures approximately 0.5 cm, representing continued diminution and approaching the upper limit of normal for size on CT. The left axillary lymph node, previously 0.6 cm, is now approximately 0.3 cm, essentially at the threshold of an incidental finding and no longer appearing convincingly pathologic. No new axillary lymph nodes are identified above normal size thresholds. There is no other lymphadenopathy in the imaged chest or axillary basins.

Chest Wall and Soft Tissues: The previously described primary site in the left posterior shoulder demonstrates expected postsurgical changes. No new subcutaneous nodules or soft tissue deposits are identified in the chest wall, bilateral flanks, or axillary subcutaneous tissues. No suspicious enhancing chest wall lesion.

Bones (Chest): No osteolytic or osteosclerotic lesions concerning for skeletal metastases.

CT ABDOMEN AND PELVIS:

Liver: There are two small residual hepatic parenchymal lesions in the right hepatic lobe, both showing substantially diminished conspicuity compared with prior studies. The more prominent of these two -- previously measuring 1.4 cm -- now measures approximately 0.6 cm in greatest dimension on the current portal venous phase images, exhibiting markedly reduced enhancement relative to prior examinations and appearing largely hypodense. The second lesion, previously measuring 0.7 cm, is now approximately 0.3 cm, representing near-complete radiographic resolution and barely perceptible as a faint hypodense focus on the current study. A third hepatic lesion that had been identified in the right lobe on the pre-immunotherapy progression scan, and which appeared absent on the prior post-immunotherapy response CT, remains unidentifiable on the current study, consistent with sustained complete resolution at that site. No new hepatic lesions are identified. No biliary ductal dilatation. The hepatic parenchyma appears otherwise homogeneous in attenuation.

Biliary System: Gallbladder is present without wall thickening, pericholecystic fluid, or intraluminal filling defect. Common bile duct is not dilated.

Spleen: The spleen is normal in size and homogeneous in attenuation. No focal splenic lesions.

Pancreas: Pancreas appears normal throughout. No pancreatic ductal dilatation. No peripancreatic lymphadenopathy or inflammatory changes.

Adrenal Glands: Both adrenal glands are normal in morphology without evidence of adrenal nodules or masses. No adrenal enlargement.

Kidneys and Ureters: Bilateral kidneys are symmetric in size and demonstrate normal parenchymal enhancement. No hydronephrosis. No stones or collecting system masses. No renal lesions of concern.

Bladder: Urinary bladder demonstrates normal wall thickness. No intraluminal filling defect.

Retroperitoneum and Mesentery: No retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymphadenopathy. No free fluid in the abdomen or pelvis.

Pelvis: Uterus and adnexa are unremarkable. No pelvic lymphadenopathy or masses. No ascites.

Gastrointestinal Tract: Bowel loops are without obstruction, wall thickening, or pneumatosis. Colonic wall morphology appears within normal limits, without evidence of residual colitis or wall thickening. This is reassuring in the context of the patient's prior grade 3 immune-mediated colitis, which has been managed with corticosteroids and is clinically in remission. No free air.

Bones (Abdomen and Pelvis): No focal osseous lesions concerning for skeletal metastatic involvement in the imaged lumbar spine, sacrum, pelvis, or proximal femora.

Soft Tissues: The right flank subcutaneous region, previously the site of metastatic subcutaneous nodularity prior to initiation of BRAF-directed therapy, shows no subcutaneous lesions whatsoever. No new soft tissue deposits anywhere in the imaged abdomen and pelvis.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

MRI BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT GADOLINIUM:

Brain Parenchyma: The brain parenchyma demonstrates normal signal intensity on all sequences. There are no areas of abnormal enhancement on post-gadolinium T1-weighted images. No parenchymal lesions are identified on FLAIR, T2-weighted, or diffusion-weighted sequences that would suggest metastatic deposits. No intra-axial or extra-axial masses.

Susceptibility-Weighted Imaging: SWI sequence demonstrates no hemorrhagic foci or hemosiderin deposits to suggest prior hemorrhagic metastases. No microbleeds.

Extra-Axial Spaces: No subdural or epidural collections. No leptomeningeal enhancement identified on post-gadolinium sequences. No abnormal pachymeningeal enhancement.

Ventricles and Sulci: Ventricles are normal in size and configuration. Sulci are appropriate for the patient's stated age. No midline shift. No evidence of hydrocephalus.

Calvarium and Skull Base: No calvarial lesions. Skull base demonstrates normal signal characteristics. Orbits, visualized paranasal sinuses, and mastoid air cells are grossly unremarkable.

Cerebrovascular: No acute infarcts on diffusion-weighted imaging. Major intracranial flow voids appear patent.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
IMPRESSION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. Continued and deepening response to ongoing nivolumab maintenance immunotherapy, with further interval diminution of all previously identified sites of disease compared with the prior CT obtained approximately 10 weeks after initiation of combination immunotherapy.

2. Bilateral axillary lymph nodes have continued to decrease in size -- the right measuring 0.5 cm and the left 0.3 cm -- approaching or within normal morphologic size limits, without convincing features of residual pathologic lymph node involvement.

3. The two residual right hepatic lobe lesions show striking further reduction: the larger now approximately 0.6 cm (previously 1.4 cm) and the smaller now approximately 0.3 cm (previously 0.7 cm). A third hepatic lesion noted at disease progression remains unidentifiable, consistent with sustained resolution. The overall hepatic disease burden now appears minimal, approaching near-complete radiographic disappearance.

4. The previously identified small right lower lobe pulmonary nodules are no longer perceptible on this examination, consistent with resolution or diminution below CT detection threshold. No new pulmonary lesions.

5. No subcutaneous nodules, chest wall disease, or new sites of metastatic involvement identified.

6. No intracranial metastatic disease on dedicated MRI brain with gadolinium contrast.

7. No imaging findings to suggest new immune-related adverse event involving thoracic or abdominal organs. Colonic wall morphology is unremarkable on CT.

Overall, the findings are consistent with a deepening and durable treatment response to checkpoint inhibitor therapy, with the hepatic metastases and pulmonary nodules approaching or achieving near-complete radiographic resolution. The findings indicate ongoing favorable response to nivolumab maintenance.

Radiologist: [Electronically signed]
Attending Radiologist, Body Imaging and Oncologic Imaging Division

Dictated and finalized.
